synopsis: The Standing Judicial Commission sustained the complaints of TE Eliot Lee against Korean Eastern Presbytery (cases 2007-09 and 2007-10). KEP erred when it empowered the Pulpit Commission to act on behalf of the church session without proper consent, and erred when it filed a civil action against TE Lee to adjudicate ecclesiastical matters. The decision affirms the non-hierarchical, voluntary nature of the PCA and its limited jurisdiction in civil matters.
